key_insight: Spacer-layer Cl doping can tune low-n square-planar nickelates to a superconductivity-relevant Ni valence, making n=2 and n=3 La-based compounds electronically resemble higher-n superconducting nickelates.
engineering_relevance: This suggests a chemically practical route to design and synthesize new low-dimensional nickelate superconductors without heavily altering the NiO2 planes.
method: DFT + DMFT to resolve layer-dependent electronic structure and correlation.
Model electron-compensated low-n La-based nickelates with spacer-layer Cl substitution.
Compare Ni-d correlation strength and low-energy Ni-d states against higher-n superconducting compounds.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that targeted n=2 and n=3 compounds develop Ni-d correlations comparable to superconducting higher-n nickelates while retaining the characteristic low-energy Ni-d electronic structure.
watch_item: The proposal is a computational prediction, so experimental feasibility, disorder from Cl substitution, and actual superconducting transition behavior still need verification.
paper_title: Layer-resolved Electronic Structure and Correlation of Low-$n$ Square-planar Nickelates: A DFT+DMFT Prediction of Superconducting Candidates
